text.title: gitops Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for gitops. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Prefer pull-based GitOps for security-focused, multi-cluster, or compliance-driven environments.
rule.rationale: Pull-based (ArgoCD, FluxCD) eliminates cluster credentials from CI pipelines — the agent inside the cluster only needs read access to the Git repo. Push-based requires exposing cluster API credentials to external CI systems, which must be rotated and audited separately.

rule.statement: Use the hybrid model (CI pushes manifest updates to Git, GitOps agent applies) for most new deployments.
rule.rationale: Hybrid combines push-model velocity for image builds and tag updates with pull-model safety for apply and drift detection. CI never has cluster credentials; the GitOps agent reconciles from Git state.

rule.statement: Use folder-per-environment, not branch-per-environment, for environment separation.
rule.rationale: Branch-based environments require cross-branch cherry-picks for config changes and make diff visibility hard. Folder-based environments (apps/{app}/overlays/{staging,prod}) keep all environments in one PR diff and enable Kustomize overlays without branching complexity.
